🎯 Management Attention

generated 2026-07-16 12:39 UTC · guidelines: docs/incident-handling-guidelines.md
Under control?No — 1 item needs immediate escalation (🚨), 7 more need management attention (🔴)
Urgent vs smaller1 urgent, 7 need a nudge, 20 to watch, 6 handled well
Total open (active, excl. triage)34 +7 vs last digest (27→34); 7 entered active (6 new, 1 accepted from triage), 0 resolved
1
🚨 Escalate now
7
🔴 Needs attention
20
🟡 Watch
6
🟢 Under control

🆕 New since last digest

  • INC-14346 — P2 CF Threat Watch brute-force-suspect alert on ft-crm.com — fast ack, escalated to security team (Max/Agustín), jointly assessed as likely benign (shared network, not an exploit); still investigating, ~29min old
  • INC-14345 — P3 Intercom Sweep Las Vegas — stopped lifecycle 13 still sent messages 12/15 Jul; lead assigned, ~31min old, too fresh to flag
  • INC-14335 — P4 RabbitMQ AP1 Maintenance — lead assigned, too fresh to flag
  • INC-14331 — P2 Leonbets activity-manager alert-spam — root cause found (Aurora DB reload storm) and fixed (RDS CPU 99%→40-50%, lag <2k); durable PR #490 not yet tracked as an incident.io follow-up
  • INC-14328 — P2 ibetcrm no-consumers — first occurrence root-caused & closed within ~1h35m (NULL consent values), recurred within the hour, reopened and being investigated
  • INC-14310 — P3 MeridianBet topic slow — lead assigned, Monitoring, resolved narrative already in incident.io summary, too fresh to flag
  • INC-14297 — P2 DMS heartbeat missing (crocobet) — was in Triage at last check, now accepted into active (not newly created); ~1h52m quiet gap between page-ack and first substantive message, incident.io summary still uninformative

✅ Resolved since last digest

  • none
Guideline codes legend (docs/incident-handling-guidelines.md)
  • A1 — Every incident has an owner and Head-of-SRE-level visibility
  • A2 — No incident is “just closed” — every problem gets an action
  • A3 — “It's just 10/30 minutes” is never an acceptable rationale
  • A4 — Recurring problems are escalated to the owning team for a long-term fix
  • B5 — Escalate early — not after hours without recovery
  • B6 — Security incidents are P1 until de-escalated
  • B7 — Critical-path failures are top priority
  • B8 — Night and handover incidents must not be lost — assign, don't just mention
  • C9 — Inform partners fast, even before root cause is known
  • C10 — Close the loop — recovery comms sent, partner questions answered
  • C11 — Comms accurate and crystal clear on impact
  • D12 — Informed decisions over passive waiting
  • D13 — Runbooks followed, basic checks done
  • D14 — Don't close while partner-side work remains open
  • D15 — The incident record is self-contained and phone-readable
  • E16 — Digest answers: under control? urgent vs smaller? new bugs? how many open?

🚨 Escalate now 1

INC-8064 slack intercomP1open 50d· evidence to 09 Jul 09:04 UTC
[P1] Intercom Incident : H2Bet Production
📝 Management note (2026-07-16) Andrea: correction — H2Bet ACCEPTED the interim workaround (discussed in the meeting with them and our Partner Manager, early Jul); that acceptance is why REW-176 was opened on 9 Jul. The 28 May rejection was the earlier 503-as-success proposal only. No sign-off is pending from H2Bet.

~300 duplicate bonus grants/week continuing after 50+ days; the accepted interim workaround (REW-176) is parked until mid-Aug by Rewards capacity, and the BetConstruct root-cause path has zero traction.

H2Bet duplicate bonus grants confirmed root cause quickly, but 50+ days later still ~300 duplicate grants/week with no fix deployed. Per management note, H2Bet accepted the interim workaround (REW-176), opened 9 Jul, but it remains blocked by Rewards capacity until mid-Aug. No change this cycle — 0 new Slack messages, 0 new Intercom parts, situation fully static, financial leakage continuing unabated.

Open breaches
  • A2/A4: no decisive fix after 50+ days, Urgent ticket parked a month+
  • B5: BetConstruct escalation zero traction
  • C10/D15: Intercom closed (since 06 Jul) implying resolution while incident.io still open P1
Proposed / pending
  • REW-176 interim workaround - ACCEPTED by H2Bet (per management note), opened Urgent 9 Jul, blocked by Rewards capacity until mid-Aug
Timeline (1)
  • No new Slack or Intercom activity this cycle

→ Decide this week: pull REW-176 forward in Rewards' queue, or formally document risk-acceptance of the leakage until mid-Aug; separately escalate the BetConstruct account-manager gap to leadership.

🔴 Needs attention 7

INC-12617 slackP2open 9d· evidence to 16 Jul 02:02 UTC
[FIRING:3] Fusion API errors Alerting (true)

Andrea's intervention today landed on the recurrence (INC-13895) rather than this original incident — worth making sure the pattern-level escalation Andrea asked for covers both.

Fusion API 401 errors; root cause ruled out as FT-side, partner informed via Teams DM 07 Jul and never re-chased since. No new Slack activity and no incident.io change at all this cycle — now roughly 9.5 days of total silence. The exact 401 pattern has recurred as INC-13895, which today got direct management attention from Andrea (see that incident) — this original occurrence has not.

Open breaches
  • C9: no partner re-chase in over 9 days
  • C10: alert cleared nearly two weeks ago, never formally closed
  • A4: recurring Fusion API 401 pattern (2nd occurrence INC-13895, now getting attention) still not escalated as a pattern from this side
Proposed / pending
  • log investigation, rule out FT-side config - done
  • inform partner - done once, never re-chased
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-07T14:19
  • last partner message unanswered
Timeline (1)
  • No new Slack messages this cycle

→ When escalating the Fusion API 401 pattern (per Andrea's direction on INC-13895), close this incident out explicitly as part of the same pattern rather than leaving it separately unresolved.

INC-13738 slackP2open 2d· evidence to 16 Jul 04:45 UTC
Betiro - No unsubs since April 2026

Unchanged since last check — the technical fix and monitor are in, but the compliance-exposure items (legal escalation, retroactive remediation, 3 new brands) still have no owner or movement.

IP-whitelisting change silently blocked unsubscribe requests for Betiro, Rivalo, Leonbets since 7 Apr - undetected ~3 months. Fix and the specifically-requested unsub-events monitor (PR #196) both landed 13-15 Jul. No new activity this cycle. Still open: no legal/compliance escalation for the ~3-month opt-out failure, no confirmed retroactive remediation, and 3 newly-flagged brands (Rocketship, Fairspin, Frank Casino) remain unaddressed.

Open breaches
  • D12: still no evidence of legal/compliance escalation for a multi-month opt-out failure across 3 gambling brands
  • C10: no confirmed retroactive remediation of missed unsubscribes
  • 3 newly-flagged brands remain unaddressed
Proposed / pending
  • Monitor landed (PR #196) - addresses detection gap
  • Open: retroactive remediation plan
  • Open: legal/compliance escalation
  • Open: extend investigation to Rocketship, Fairspin, Frank Casino
Timeline (1)
  • No new Slack messages this cycle

→ Escalate the opt-out failure to legal/compliance given regulatory exposure; confirm/execute retroactive remediation; open tracking for the 3 newly-flagged brands before treating this as closed.

INC-13895 slackP2open 2d· evidence to 16 Jul 10:51 UTC
[FIRING:1] Fusion API errors Alerting (spinbet-terragon: 401 @ /external/pusher/:brand true)

Downgraded 🚨→🔴: the 'no escalation in sight' gap is resolved — Andrea has now personally stepped in with clear direction — but the underlying technical mismatch is still unresolved and the partner still has an inaccurate account, so this still needs a concrete follow-through this cycle.

Fusion API 401 errors blocked on-site notifications/Rich Inbox for Spinbet Terragon — the 2nd occurrence of this alert family in a week (see INC-12617). After days of silence following Joachim's 15 Jul re-chase, Andrea personally joined the channel today (12:48 CEST) and directed Joachim to investigate further whether a new origin/setting is causing the mismatch, and to correct the partner comms with the real impact.

Open breaches
  • A4: 2nd Fusion API 401 incident in a week, still not escalated to the owning team as a pattern
  • C9/C11: partner comms known to be inaccurate on impact, correction directed but not yet sent
Proposed / pending
  • Partner asked to check the API exchange and 401 cause on their end - no response received
  • Andrea's direction: investigate new origin/setting mismatch, fix partner comms - just assigned, not yet actioned
Partner view
  • last partner message unanswered
  • will update when more information available - still no follow-up sent to partner
Timeline (4)
  • Morning 15 Jul Joachim re-chases the partner - no reply since
  • 20:22 CEST 15 Jul and prior bot 'mark as resolved?' nudges, unanswered
  • 16 Jul 08:10 CEST Incident Lead reassigned Nazareno -> Joachim (administrative only)
  • 16 Jul 12:48-12:51 CEST Andrea Azzopardi joins the channel directly, tells Joachim this needs more investigation into a possible new origin/setting mismatch, and that partner comms need correcting with the real impact

→ Joachim to report back on the new origin/setting investigation and send the corrected partner comms Andrea asked for.

INC-13917 slack intercomP2open 2d· evidence to 16 Jul 01:21 UTC
Campeon: Slow processing on credit service (from lifecycles)

The partner side is now fully resolved and closed, which makes the still-blank incident.io record more conspicuous, not less — there's a real risk this gets forgotten precisely because the visible (Intercom) side already looks done.

Campeon lifecycle coin-crediting delayed up to 2h on 11 Jul, root cause auto-claim + serialized credit path. An 8xlarge instance upgrade completed as short-term mitigation (~13:35 CEST 15 Jul); partner was told and confirmed via Intercom, ticket formally closed 16 Jul (rated by partner). No new Slack activity this cycle. incident.io itself has never been updated at all — still shows 'Investigating' with zero status updates despite the mitigation, partner comms, and even the Intercom-side closure having all already happened.

Open breaches
  • D15: incident.io still shows zero status updates and 'Investigating' even though the partner-facing Intercom ticket is already closed and CSAT-rated — the internal record is now behind the partner-facing one
  • C11: partner told a generic 'performance bottleneck'; internally-confirmed root cause still not disclosed
  • D14: permanent fix (credit VC directly vs auto-claim) still undecided while the record trends toward looking closed
Proposed / pending
  • Temporary: scale credit-service compute to 8xlarge - completed
  • Permanent: credit VC directly vs auto-claim bonus - undecided
  • Add audit trail for Rewards bonus-config edits - raised, not tracked
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-14T11:18
  • Rewards team confirmed a performance-bottleneck backlog - partial/generic explanation
  • Infrastructure upgrade confirmed complete to partner - delivered, ticket closed
Timeline (3)
  • ~13:35 CEST 15 Jul 8xlarge instance upgrade completed
  • Intercom ticket closed and CSAT-rated 16 Jul (~09:16 UTC) — partner side is fully wrapped up
  • incident.io still shows zero status updates and status 'Investigating'

→ Post the overdue incident.io status update now that the partner ticket has closed, and make an explicit decision on the permanent fix (direct VC credit vs auto-claim) rather than leaving it open indefinitely.

failed to resolve container IP after 3 attempts

Unchanged — the textbook long-runner-with-no-action-plan case: a confirmed root cause and cheap fix list sitting untouched on a recurring failure class.

→ Escalate to Jeremy/AI Team lead directly to get at minimum the immediate env-only mitigation actioned this week.

INC-13664 slack intercomP3open 3d· evidence to 15 Jul 23:15 UTC
[P3] Intercom Incident : SBTSolutions Production

Today's exchange resolved the partner's confusion about scope, but the original comms-integrity issue — telling the partner work was 'scheduled' when it had already been done — is still uncorrected, and the incident is one auto-nudge away from closing on that inaccurate record.

SBTSolutions reported delayed bonus/activity-trigger processing; Matteo migrated the messaging system (RabbitMQ→Kafka) without partner sign-off, then told the partner it would be 'scheduled' — misrepresenting work already done. New this cycle: the partner (Gergana) pushed back via Intercom, confused because their own integration uses Kafka/REST, not RabbitMQ. Matteo clarified it's a purely internal FT backend migration with no partner action needed — but incident.io is still 'Waiting on Partner' and no accurate resolution message has been sent covering the original misrepresentation.

Open breaches
  • D15: stuck 'Waiting on Partner' since 13 Jul
  • C11: original message telling the partner the migration would be 'scheduled' (when already applied) still never corrected — today's clarification addressed a different point (which messaging system) not this one
  • C10: no resolution/closing message sent to the partner despite the fix being considered done internally
Proposed / pending
  • Migrate to Kafka (root fix) - implemented, now clarified to partner as internal-only
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-11T18:40
  • Kafka migration during low-traffic window, no partner action needed - clarified 16 Jul after partner confusion; original 'scheduled' misrepresentation still not corrected
Timeline (2)
  • 16 Jul 09:15 UTC partner (Gergana) asks for clarification, noting their integration doesn't use RabbitMQ
  • 16 Jul 09:52 UTC Matteo clarifies this is an internal FT messaging migration only, no partner action needed

→ Send the partner an accurate resolution message (migration completed, not 'scheduled') before moving incident.io to Closed.

No new activity this cycle — now roughly 24 hours of quiet with no further logout reports, well into the planned ~2-day observation window before closing.

Management note 16 Jul: solved as an incident; remaining work is the impacted-player cross-check with Estelarbet. No change this cycle since Andrea's note was written.

Cloudflare-origin security check blocked legitimate shortlink/unsubscribe traffic on 2 Jul; mitigated within ~2h, root-cause fix merged same evening. Partner (Estelarbet/PLIN) has been waiting on a promised RCA report through several broken promises; no new Slack or Intercom activity this cycle.

INC-10045 slack intercomP2open 27d· evidence to 16 Jul 10:03 UTC
[P2] Intercom Incident : Power Play Production

This is exactly the right move (comprehensive handover instead of another manual re-sync) — just needs Andrea's sign-off to actually go out and create the tracked ownership transfer.

GiG (Power Play's upstream KYC) event-ordering bug leaves stale block tags. Matteo drafted a comprehensive handover today: a partner-facing message (explaining the recurring same-second collisions and handing ownership to Integration/PM teams) plus a structured internal handover memo covering root cause, what SRE has done, and the two structural fix options — and asked Andrea directly 'what do you think?'. Awaiting Andrea's go-ahead to send/action it.

Open breaches
  • C10: the drafted handover directly addresses the previously-flagged untracked-follow-up gap, but hasn't been sent/actioned yet — still pending Andrea's decision
Proposed / pending
  • historical re-sync - done multiple rounds
  • update_epoch as ordering key - drafted handover identifies this as a candidate fix for Integration/dev to assess, with an honest feasibility writeup
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-06-16T12:16
  • notify once re-syncs complete - kept
  • structural handover to Integration/PM drafted 16 Jul - awaiting Andrea's go-ahead to send
Timeline (1)
  • 16 Jul 10:03 CEST Matteo posts a fully-drafted partner message and an internal SRE→Integration/PM handover memo (root cause, options, open decisions), asks Andrea for sign-off

→ Andrea to review and approve Matteo's drafted partner message and Integration/PM handover so this stops being an SRE-carried recurring task.

INC-11079 slackP2open 19d· evidence to 15 Jul 10:46 UTC
segurobet: no consumers on queue segurobet.consentsmanager
📝 Management note (2026-07-16) Andrea: this is in reality a BetConstruct platform issue (their Operator API returns 500 on consent updates). The partner is aware and has raised it with BetConstruct themselves; active partner correspondence (examples provided, regular chasing incl. by Andrea) lives in the partner channel thread: https://fasttrack-solutions.slack.com/archives/C08VB1RBJLF/p1782549375502499 — future runs should treat that thread as the live track, not the quiet incident channel.

Per management note: BetConstruct-side root cause, active partner correspondence, blocked on the vendor not FT inaction. Andrea's fresh re-chase today confirms the cadence is being kept up as intended.

Segurobet Consent Manager queue stalled since 27 Jun due to partner's (BetConstruct/Betco) Operator API 500s. Per Andrea's management note, the live track is the partner channel thread (#ftcrm-segurobet), not the quiet incident channel. Andrea personally re-chased BetConstruct again today (12:54 CEST) in that thread, continuing the active chase cadence — no reply yet.

Open breaches
  • External blocker: BetConstruct 'at a loss' — no ETA; Andrea is actively maintaining the chase cadence
Proposed / pending
  • notify partner + supply failing examples - done
  • no FT-side workaround proposed
  • zero incident.io follow-ups exist
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-06-27T08:36
  • 'we'll check' (01 Jul) - no ETA ever given; Andrea re-chased again 16 Jul
Timeline (2)
  • 16 Jul 12:54 CEST Andrea Azzopardi re-chases BetConstruct (Eduardo Nunes) directly in the partner thread: 'Are there any updates from Betconstruct about this issue please?' — no reply yet
  • No new activity in the main incident Slack channel this cycle

→ Consider an FT-side mitigation while BetConstruct investigates: locally suppress Email/SMS sends for players whose opt-out failed to sync (D12) — removes the compliance exposure without waiting on the vendor.

INC-12998 slackP2open 6d· evidence to 16 Jul 10:47 UTC
frank-casino: deployment ping-pong is stuck on CrashLoopBackOff
📝 Management note (2026-07-16) Andrea: the deployment ping-pong service is not critical — a crash-loop here is not a critical-path failure (B7 does not apply). Watch-level, not escalate.

Watch-level per management note (ping-pong is non-critical, B7 doesn't apply) — and now has real forward motion: Andrea's own diagnostic work found a concrete error signature (403 on ping) after ~6 days of no diagnostic at all.

frank-casino ping-pong deployment stuck in CrashLoopBackOff. Andrea Azzopardi personally joined the channel today (12:42 CEST) and posted diagnostic logs showing a concrete new lead: the pod is fataling on 'code [403] received from integration API on ping request'. Andrea asked Giancarlo for a summary when he has time, explicitly noting he's aware this isn't a critical service.

Open breaches
  • D15: incident.io summary field still blank; the 403 finding is in Slack only, not yet in incident.io
Timeline (1)
  • 16 Jul 12:42-12:47 CEST Andrea joins the channel, posts pod logs showing a 403 from the integration API on the ping request, asks Giancarlo for a summary 'when you have time'

→ Giancarlo to follow up on Andrea's 403-integration-API finding and post a summary to incident.io.

INC-13757 slackP2open 2d· evidence to 16 Jul 09:03 UTC
Campeon: Incorrect games mappings

Real, continued engineering progress with proper incident.io writeups — the only open item is the administrative lead field, plus a small open decision (remap vs. alias cleanup) that just needs someone to pick.

Continued progress on Campeon's brand-wide game-mapping mismatches. Samuela fixed two more games this morning (Coins of Ra reconciled, Big Bass slug corrected), bringing the residual count down to 87 — all disconnected from active challenges, so no further player impact. All 87 have alias-pollution issues; Samuela is asking Hans (Greco) and Linus whether to clean up aliases properly or just manually re-map since impact is low, awaiting their decision.

Open breaches
  • B8/A1: incident.io Incident Lead still shows Daniel Lindengren (on vacation) despite Samuela having driven 100% of the visible engineering work
Proposed / pending
  • Manually re-map remaining 87 games (low impact, no active challenges) - proposed, awaiting Hans/Linus decision
  • Clean up alias pollution properly at the source - alternative, awaiting decision
Timeline (1)
  • 16 Jul 11:01 CEST Samuela: two more games fixed, 87 residual games remain (none affecting active challenges), all have alias-pollution issues, asks Hans/Linus for a decision on remap vs alias cleanup

→ Update incident.io Incident Lead to Samuela Lundborg; Hans/Linus to make the remap-vs-cleanup call so the remaining 87 games get closed out.

INC-14151 slack intercomP2open 23h· evidence to 16 Jul 06:52 UTC
[P2] Intercom Incident : MeridianBet Production

The right actions were taken (real fix applied, partner asked to verify), but the verification request has now gone unanswered for about 6 hours and the incident.io record still describes the wrong (superseded) fix.

MeridianBet couldn't add sports bonuses; Hikmet found the real root cause (hidden required AuthType field) and fixed it directly. Joachim asked the partner via Intercom at 08:39 UTC to re-check if it's resolved. No reply from the partner and no new Slack/Intercom activity since — the re-check request has now been outstanding for ~6 hours.

Open breaches
  • D15: incident.io's logged update still describes the superseded first FE fix, not the real root cause
  • C10: partner re-check request now ~6 hours unanswered; second user's related failure still unconfirmed as covered
Proposed / pending
  • Actual fix (Hikmet): remove 'required' flag from AuthType field - applied, unverified with partner
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-15T11:58
  • last partner message unanswered
  • Partner re-check requested 08:39 UTC 16 Jul - no reply after ~6 hours
Timeline (3)
  • 15 Jul 19:34 UTC Hikmet finds and fixes the real root cause (AuthType field required+hidden, no default)
  • 16 Jul 08:36-08:39 CEST Joachim confirms no deployment needed, asks partner (Jamie) via Intercom to re-check
  • No reply or further activity since (checked again 14:39 CEST — ~6h outstanding)

→ Send one follow-up nudge to the partner if no reply by end of day; update incident.io with the real root cause regardless of partner reply timing.

INC-14177 slackP2open 20h· evidence to 16 Jul 06:25 UTC
campeon: no consumers on queue campeon.actions.callbackconsumer (messages: 1, in: 0/s)

Very low technical impact (1 message), but the promised morning partner push from over 6 hours ago hasn't shown up in the record — either it happened off-platform again (repeating the D15 gap) or it hasn't happened yet.

A single stuck message on the campeon callback-consumer queue triggered a P2 alert; James acknowledged instantly but the incident sat in Triage ~1h43m before moving to Investigating, then jumped straight to Waiting on Partner via a colleague's off-platform Teams relay. Lead reassigned twice overnight, now Joachim, who said this morning (08:24 CEST) he'd 'send a push this morning' to the partner — no new channel activity since, so as of this check (14:39 CEST) that push has not visibly happened.

Open breaches
  • D15: record not self-contained - partner comms happened off-platform via a colleague with no content/timestamp captured
  • B5: original ~1h43m quiet gap between alert and investigation start
  • C9: the morning partner push Joachim committed to at 08:24 CEST has not appeared in-channel roughly 6 hours later
Proposed / pending
  • Escalate to partner via Teams (delegated to Gian) - done, but undocumented in-channel
Partner view
  • last partner message unanswered
  • Partner message relayed via Teams by Gian on James's behalf - content/timestamp not captured in the incident channel
  • Joachim: partner push planned this morning (16 Jul) - still not visible in-channel as of 14:39 CEST
Timeline (5)
  • 15 Jul 16:44 UTC alert fires (1 message stuck), James acknowledges within seconds
  • 15 Jul 18:27 UTC (~1h43m later) incident accepted, Triage -> Investigating
  • 15 Jul 18:28 UTC moved to Waiting on Partner; partner message relayed via Teams by a colleague on James's behalf
  • 16 Jul 05:54-08:10 CEST Incident Lead reassigned James -> Nazareno -> Joachim Farrugia
  • 16 Jul 08:24 CEST Joachim: 'Waiting on them' (partner); 'Will send a push this morning' — no further channel activity since

→ Joachim to confirm in-channel whether the promised partner push was sent, and if so capture what was said.

INC-14297 slackP2open 5h· evidence to 16 Jul 09:33 UTC
DMS: ftcrm-crocobet-ds-player-activity-calculator-default is missing

P2 severity with a real quiet gap and a record that can't be understood from the phone alone — the DMS heartbeat context suggests low player impact, but the record doesn't say so explicitly.

DMS heartbeat-failure alert for crocobet's player-activity calculator. Joachim's page was acknowledged instantly (09:15:32 CEST), but the incident then sat with only one substantive message ('Has been completed', 11:07:59 CEST) before being formally accepted straight to Monitoring at 11:33:50 CEST — a ~2h18m gap between ack and acceptance. The incident.io summary was never filled in beyond the auto-generated 'created by heartbeat-api', so it's unclear from the record alone what broke or what was completed.

Open breaches
  • B5: ~1h52m quiet gap between page acknowledgment and the first substantive message
  • D15: incident.io summary still reads only 'created by heartbeat-api' — no description of what was missing or what was fixed
Timeline (4)
  • 16 Jul 07:15 UTC alert fires, Joachim acknowledges within seconds
  • 09:07 CEST Joachim posts an image captioned 'Has been completed 🤔' — the only substantive content in the channel
  • 09:33 CEST incident formally accepted, Triage → Monitoring (skipping Investigating)
  • No further activity since

→ Joachim to add a one-line summary of what the heartbeat failure meant and what 'has been completed' refers to, so the record is self-contained.

INC-14328 slackP2open 2h· evidence to 16 Jul 12:25 UTC
ibetcrm: no consumers on queue ibetcrm.activitymanager.fireinstance (messages: 52, in: 0/s)

Strong diagnostic work both times, and the recurrence is being actively investigated immediately — but closing and reopening within an hour on the same alert family is exactly the kind of pattern management wants visibility on if it happens a third time.

ibetcrm Activity Manager Fire service stopped consuming its queue. Joachim root-caused it well within ~1h30m (NULL values in analytics.user_consents.post_mail crashing the bool parser — 557 of 674,216 rows for brand 131), fixed the data, and the incident was resolved and closed at 13:39 CEST. It recurred within the hour: a new alert (31 messages) fired and auto-merged in at 14:24 CEST, reopening the incident to Investigating. Joachim is actively investigating the recurrence.

Open breaches
  • A4: closed as resolved once already today, then recurred within the hour — worth confirming the same root cause (or a related NULL-data pattern in another brand) before closing again
Proposed / pending
  • NULL→0 data fix for brand 131 - applied, held for ~45min then the same alert class recurred
Timeline (4)
  • 16 Jul 12:04 UTC alert fires, Joachim acknowledges within seconds
  • 12:30-13:34 CEST root cause confirmed (NULL post_mail values) and fixed (set to 0); coordinated a temporary image revert with Renato Caldara along the way
  • 13:38-13:39 CEST incident.io updated with full root-cause writeup, resolved and closed
  • 14:24-14:25 CEST issue recurs (new alert, 31 messages, auto-merged as INC-14341); Joachim reopens to Investigating: 'Issue re-occurred again. Investigating this further'

→ Before closing again, confirm whether this is the same brand/cause recurring or a new one, and consider a data-validation guard for consents.post_mail if NULLs keep appearing.

INC-14331 slackP2open 2h· evidence to 16 Jul 12:39 UTC
Leonbets Activity Manager Groups Alerts Spamming

Excellent technical handling — real root cause, quantified impact, working mitigation within ~90 minutes — but the durable half of the fix (merging the PR, persisting config) isn't yet tracked as a follow-up, and today's kubectl changes are explicitly noted to revert on the next deploy.

Leonbets activity-manager and activity-manager-lifecycles consumer groups were spamming slow-processing alerts (SOC noise), traced by Matteo to a shared Aurora writer being saturated by full-brand config reloads (leonbets has 598k actions, ~7.9M rows read per pod per reload). Matteo deployed a temporary kubectl-level fix (scoped action loading + longer reload interval), cutting RDS CPU from 94-99% to 40-50% and lag from ~94k/77k to <2k on each group within ~90 minutes of investigation starting. Set to Monitoring at 12:16:59 CEST.

Open breaches
  • A2: no incident.io follow-up yet tracking the durable fix (merge PR #490 + persist the two EV settings) — the applied fix reverts on the next deploy if this isn't done
Proposed / pending
  • Scoped action loading (PR #490) - applied via kubectl today, durable merge+EV persistence still pending
  • Lifecycles reload interval 120s→300s - applied, durable EV persistence still pending
Timeline (5)
  • 16 Jul 10:47 UTC alert fires (pair of alerts, 2 related incidents auto-merged)
  • 12:47-13:20 CEST Matteo root-causes: DB saturation from unscoped config reload, not a Kafka/consumer issue; two prior scale-up attempts (partitions, pod count) made it worse
  • 13:20-14:09 CEST Matteo drafts and deploys PR #490 (scoped action loading) as a kubectl-level change, plus bumped lifecycles reload interval to 300s
  • 14:16:59 CEST incident updated with full root-cause writeup and measured impact; status → Monitoring
  • Explicit note in the update: today's kubectl-level changes revert on the next OneClick deploy unless PR #490 is merged and the env vars persisted

→ Create an incident.io follow-up for merging PR #490 and persisting the two EV settings before this incident closes, so the fix survives the next OneClick deploy.

INC-5630 slackP3open 77d· evidence to 15 Jul 10:46 UTC
Coinbar SMS Issues with Credit
📝 Management note (2026-07-16) Andrea: known issue with the partner relating to credit — the partner is aware and we are awaiting their further feedback. Watch-level, not escalate.

Per management note: known partner credit issue, FT awaiting their feedback — blocked on the partner, not FT inaction.

SMS distributor service has not processed queued messages for all Coinbar brands since before 30 Apr - over 1M messages backlogged. Per management note, this is a known credit-related issue with the partner, who is aware; FT is awaiting their feedback. No new activity this cycle.

Open breaches
  • Awaiting partner feedback (credit issue) — keep a periodic chase cadence so the wait stays deliberate
Timeline (1)
  • No new Slack activity this cycle

→ Set a chase rhythm with the partner (e.g. weekly) and record each nudge in the incident; if their credit situation won't resolve, decide deliberately whether to purge/park the backlog and close.

INC-10810 slackP3open 21d· evidence to 16 Jul 02:02 UTC
12play brands fire late

Substance well in hand — purely a record-hygiene gap, but a real handover risk while someone on a month's leave is listed as owner.

12play/we88 (Momentum) players received rewards 45-50min late due to RDS overload; a real fix landed (4 PRs, 2 DB upgrades). Handover from Daniel (on leave) to Sam Lundborg happened in Slack but incident.io still lists Daniel as Incident Lead and status unchanged at 'Fixing' since 10 Jul. No new activity this cycle.

Open breaches
  • D15: incident.io status stuck 'Fixing' and Incident Lead field still shows Daniel Lindengren (on leave)
Proposed / pending
  • RDS/cluster upgrade maintenance window scheduled for next Wednesday with the partner
  • RabbitMQ 4.x cutover coordination now owned by Sam Lundborg
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-06-24T16:00
  • update 12play via Intercom on fix progress - discussed internally, completion not confirmed
Timeline (1)
  • No new Slack activity this cycle

→ Sam Lundborg to formally reassign Incident Lead to himself ahead of the scheduled maintenance window.

INC-12754 slackP3open 8d· evidence to 15 Jul 13:34 UTC
Campeon Nexto unable to login

Fix is solid and no recurrence reported, but the incident continues to drift without a formal close.

Campeon Nexto users couldn't log in due to a v2/v3 URL-version mismatch. Fix held since 08 Jul; incident.io has sat in 'Fixing' P3 status for 8+ days with no substantive activity this cycle either.

Open breaches
  • D15/C10: incident.io still stuck in 'Fixing' P3 status over a week after the fix was confirmed working, no closing note
Timeline (1)
  • No new Slack activity this cycle

→ Have Giancarlo Laferla post a short confirmation update and move status to Fixed/Closed.

INC-14284 slackP3open 7h· evidence to 16 Jul 09:13 UTC
Daily Data Science Message Spikes

Well-diagnosed and confirmed no partner impact, which resolves the immediate concern — but this is a known recurring daily pattern with no ETA on the actual fix, exactly the kind of thing guideline A4 wants escalated to a committed backlog item rather than re-alerting indefinitely.

Daily 7-8:30AM/19:00-20:45 Data Science message spikes across multiple brands (SlotV, Crocobet, Fairspin, Corestar, Roobet — 50 alerts in 5 days). Clayton (Data Science) confirmed to Joachim and Andrea (both directly engaged) that this is expected heavy Singularity/Argo batch computation with no partner-facing impact, and that adding pods won't help since the jobs themselves are heavy. No ETA exists for lightening the queries; it's in the backlog.

Open breaches
  • A4: confirmed daily recurring pattern (this is at least the 2nd digest cycle it's persisted across) with no committed fix date, just 'in the backlog'
Proposed / pending
  • Lighten Data Science queries - identified as the real fix, no ETA, in backlog
Timeline (4)
  • 16 Jul 05:23 UTC alert fires (recurring pattern, auto-merged from 2 related incidents)
  • 09:38-09:44 CEST Joachim shares 5-day trend data with Clayton/Jeremy, asks about partner impact and mitigation options
  • 11:09-11:10 CEST Clayton: confirmed no partner-facing impact, heavy expected computation, pod scaling won't help; cc'd Andrea
  • 11:10 CEST Joachim: 'no ETA on this and is in the backlog' for lightening the queries

→ Get a backlog priority/ETA from Data Science for the query-lightening work, or formally accept the daily alert noise and tune the alert thresholds instead.

INC-14301 slackP3open 4h· evidence to 16 Jul 09:49 UTC
Star Casino NL: queue star-casino-nl.actions.bonusdistributor is processing slow (messages: 25904, in: 0/s, delivery: 0/s)

Quick initial response, but the queue has been clear for 4+ hours with the record never updated to reflect it — exactly the silent-drift-toward-auto-close pattern the guidelines flag, on an incident whose underlying cause (partner's own site outage) was never confirmed resolved either.

Star Casino NL bonus-distributor queue backlog (25,904 messages). Giancarlo accepted and sent partner comms within ~20 minutes, noting the partner's own website was down. Both attached alerts have since resolved (queue drained) as of 11:49 CEST, but incident.io status has not moved off 'Investigating' since 08:29:46 CEST — over 4 hours with no closing update despite the underlying issue clearing.

Open breaches
  • C10/D15: both underlying alerts resolved by 11:49 CEST but incident.io still shows 'Investigating' over 4 hours later with no closing update or detail on what the sent comms said
Partner view
  • comms sent 08:29 CEST - content/recipient not detailed in-channel (D15)
Timeline (4)
  • 16 Jul 08:09 UTC alert fires (25,904-message backlog)
  • 08:29 CEST Giancarlo accepts, notes partner's website is down, sends comms
  • 09:24 CEST first attached alert resolves; 11:49 CEST second attached alert resolves — queue fully drained
  • No status update since 08:33:57 CEST despite both alerts clearing

→ Giancarlo to post a closing update confirming the backlog cleared and whether the partner's own outage (root cause) has been confirmed resolved.

INC-10938 slackP4open 20d
Security incident: Malicious dashboard creation attempt

private — review directly

INC-13889 slack intercomP4open 2d· evidence to 15 Jul 18:03 UTC
Calendars: Orphan (but live) door variations in the database(!)

Low real risk (no player impact, PRs in flight), but this remains the silent-closure pattern — unowned record, unanswered auto-resolve prompt, now getting stale.

12Play's Chinese comms profile appeared missing from the backoffice — a display bug only, no player-facing impact. Rewards team opened two PRs 14 Jul; no Incident Lead role has ever been formally assigned. No new activity this cycle — the bot's 15 Jul stale-incident nudge remains unanswered, now ~19 hours.

Open breaches
  • D15/A1: incident.io still has no summary set and no formally assigned Incident Lead; the bot's 15 Jul auto-resolve nudge is unanswered ~19 hours
Proposed / pending
  • backoffice PR - opened 14 Jul, pending test
  • rewards-backend PR - opened 14 Jul, pending test
  • handoff to Sam Lundborg to verify both PRs
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-10T04:15
  • fix release tomorrow/Thursday - not yet confirmed done
Timeline (1)
  • No new Slack or Intercom activity this cycle

→ Sam Lundborg to formally take the Incident Lead role, add a one-line summary, and confirm the two PRs' merge status before letting the bot resolve this.

🟢 Under control 6

6 handled well: WorkOS/CRM-login P1 outage (14303) recovering cleanly with fast, repeated partner comms; CF Threat Watch brute-force alert (14346) triaged fast and jointly assessed as benign; Rhino Intercom bug (14168) root cause now communicated directly to the partner; Sweep Las Vegas (14345), RabbitMQ AP1 maintenance (14335) and MeridianBet slow-topic (14310) too fresh/already-resolved to flag.

INC-14303 slackP1open 4h· evidence to 16 Jul 12:34 UTC
All CRM Log In Not Available

Fast, correct, and sustained handling of an all-brands P1: instant ack, 1-minute root cause, repeated proactive partner comms as the picture evolved, and a residual partner-facing symptom (delayed codes) diagnosed and resolved within 45 minutes of being raised. No breaches — still worth a final closing confirmation once WorkOS's own backlog fully clears.

CRM login unavailable across all brands due to a WorkOS (3rd-party auth) outage. Instant ack, root cause in ~1 minute, Patrik and Andrea both actively driving response throughout. Partial recovery noticed ~12:09 CEST with most brands recovering; comms sent to partners twice (12:11 and 12:19 CEST). One partner-facing residual issue (login codes delayed by the WorkOS backlog, 7-9min) surfaced at 13:49 CEST via a new partner (CasinoOK) report and was resolved by 14:34 CEST with clear guidance (wait ~10min, use only the latest code). Status moved to Monitoring at 12:26 CEST.

Proposed / pending
  • Wait on WorkOS to recover (external dependency) - largely resolved
  • Google-signin feature-flag fallback for affected brands - discussed, not needed once WorkOS recovered
  • Guidance for residual login-code delays (wait ~10min, use latest code only) - given directly to affected partner
Partner view
  • In-app/Intercom comms sent twice (10:11 and 10:19 UTC) as recovery progressed - kept
Timeline (7)
  • 16 Jul 08:13 UTC alert fires, Joachim acknowledges instantly, root cause (WorkOS) confirmed within ~1 minute
  • 10:03-10:12 CEST Andrea: 'Issue still ongoing'; discussion of Google-signin feature-flag fallback for affected brands
  • 10:04-10:05 CEST Andrea: '6 partners reached out through Partner Support'
  • 10:09-10:21 CEST users/brands start reporting recovery (Carolina, Linus, Fabio); Ekaterina flags Tanzania/Slott still 100% down, Leonbets 50/50
  • 10:11-10:19 CEST Patrik + Andrea agree comms wording ('seeing it working', wait for provider confirmation); Andrea posts 'Comms out' at 10:19:42 UTC
  • 10:26:08 CEST incident.io status Investigating → Monitoring
  • 11:47-12:34 CEST partner (CasinoOK, via Daria) reports not receiving login codes; Felipe confirms a residual ~7-9min WorkOS-side sending delay, advises 'request once, wait 10min, use the latest code'; confirmed resolved for that partner by 12:34 CEST
INC-14168 slack intercomP2open 21h· evidence to 16 Jul 07:52 UTC
[P2] Intercom Incident : Rhino Production

Both previously-flagged gaps (root cause undocumented, partner uninformed) are now closed — partner has a clear, accurate explanation and remedy. Worth a quick incident.io update to record the root cause formally, but no breach remains.

Rhino reported via Intercom that the Save button stays disabled when editing unsubscribe-page HTML. Front-end (Carl) found the root cause this morning (new template validation blocking script tags), and Matteo has now communicated the root cause and remedy directly to the partner (Dario) via Intercom: removing the flagged script section restores the Save button. The earlier C10/D15 gap (root cause found but not communicated) is now closed.

Proposed / pending
  • Partner removes the offending script tag from their own template - explained, awaiting confirmation it resolves it
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-15T14:06
  • Root cause + remedy explained to partner 16 Jul 12:25 CEST - delivered
Timeline (4)
  • 15 Jul 14:06 UTC Rhino reports the bug via Intercom bot
  • 16 Jul 08:14-09:04 CEST Joachim escalates to front-end; Carl finds likely root cause (script tags now blocked in the template)
  • 16 Jul 07:40 UTC partner (Dario) confirms the earlier silence was fine given low urgency
  • 16 Jul 12:25 CEST Matteo explains the root cause and the fix (remove the flagged script tag) directly to the partner via Intercom
INC-14346 slackP2open 41m· evidence to 16 Jul 12:39 UTC
CF Threat Watch: brute-force-suspect,login-success-after-fails on ft-crm.com (41.76.35.208)

Fast ack and correct B6 security escalation flow (SOC→SRE→security team), even though the paging itself misfired (Max unavailable, Agustín's escalation expired) — both engaged within ~4 minutes regardless. Too fresh and too well-handled to flag; current evidence points to a false positive.

CF Threat Watch flagged a brute-force-suspect pattern (401×37 then 40×2xx SUCCESS logins) from a single IP on ft-crm.com. Joachim acknowledged instantly; escalation to the security on-call (Max, Agustín) had paging misses (Max unavailable, Agustín's escalation expired) but both joined the channel manually within ~4 minutes anyway via Stephen Riolo, who took over as lead. Team is jointly investigating via Cloudflare security analytics; current read is a shared-network false positive, not an attack — CF itself classifies it as 'not an exploit'.

Proposed / pending
  • Verify via Cloudflare security analytics + bot session double-check - in progress
Timeline (4)
  • 16 Jul 12:10:44 UTC alert fires, Joachim acknowledges within seconds
  • 12:11:36-12:14:59 UTC escalation to security on-call: Max paged but unavailable, Agustín's escalation expired, Stephen Riolo acknowledges and becomes lead; Max and Agustín both join the channel regardless
  • 12:21-12:39 UTC joint investigation via Cloudflare dashboard; Agustín: 'doesn't look like an attack and CF is classifying it as not an exploit'; Stephen: 'don't think this is an attack... more some shared network'
  • Still actively investigating at check time (~29min old)
INC-14310P3open 4h
🔥🧯 MeridianBet: topic meridianbet.integration is processing slow (lag: 1031799) group: crm-integration-consumer-meridianbet

MeridianBet integration topic slow-processing alert, lead Joachim assigned, status Monitoring with a recent update. Not selected for full deep-read (P3, has a lead, updated recently).

INC-14345P3open 43m
[P3] Intercom Incident : Sweep Las Vegas Production

Sweep Las Vegas partner reported players still receiving messages from lifecycle 13 on 12/15 Jul despite it being marked stopped on 9 Jul. Lead assigned (Joachim), ~31min old at check time — not selected for full deep-read (P3, has a lead, no other trigger criteria met).

INC-14335P4open 85m
RabbitMQ AP1 Maintenance

RabbitMQ AP1 planned maintenance, reported by Matteo, lead Joachim assigned. ~1h14m old at check time, low severity — not selected for full deep-read.
